006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with

008 *

016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.marshall.html;
018
019import org.apache.juneau.commons.collections.*;
020import org.apache.juneau.marshall.*;
021import org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.*;
022import org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.*;
023
024/**
025 * Serializes POJO metamodels to HTML.
026 *
027 * <h5 class='topic'>Media types</h5>
028 *
029 * Handles <c>Accept</c> types:  <bc>text/html+schema</bc>
030 * <p>
031 * Produces <c>Content-Type</c> types:  <bc>text/html</bc>
032 *
033 * <h5 class='topic'>Description</h5>
034 *
035 * Essentially the same as {@link HtmlSerializer}, except serializes the POJO metamodel instead of the model itself.
036 *
037 * <p>
038 * Produces output that describes the POJO metamodel similar to an XML schema document.
039 *
040 * <p>
041 * The easiest way to create instances of this class is through the {@link HtmlSerializer#getSchemaSerializer()},
042 * which will create a schema serializer with the same settings as the originating serializer.
043 *
044 * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
045 *    <li class='note'>This class is thread safe and reusable.
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
049 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/HtmlSupport">HTML Basics</a>
050
051 * </ul>
052 */
053@SuppressWarnings({
054   "java:S110", // Inheritance depth acceptable for HtmlSchemaSerializer hierarchy
055   "java:S115"  // Constants use UPPER_snakeCase convention (e.g., PROP_generator)
056})
057public class HtmlSchemaSerializer extends HtmlSerializer {
058
059   // Property name constants
060   private static final String PROP_generator = "generator";
061
062   /**
063    * Builder class.
064    */
065   public static class Builder extends HtmlSerializer.Builder<Builder> {
066
067      private static final Cache<HashKey,HtmlSchemaSerializer> CACHE = Cache.of(HashKey.class, HtmlSchemaSerializer.class).build();
068
069      JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder generatorBuilder;
070
071      /**
072       * Constructor, default settings.
073       */
074      protected Builder() {
075         produces("text/html");
076         accept("text/html+schema");
077         generatorBuilder = JsonSchemaGenerator.create().marshallingContext(marshallingContext());
078      }
079
080      /**
081       * Copy constructor.
082       *
083       * @param copyFrom The builder to copy from.
084       */
085      protected Builder(Builder copyFrom) {
086         super(copyFrom);
087         generatorBuilder = copyFrom.generatorBuilder.copy().marshallingContext(marshallingContext());
088      }
089
090      /**
091       * Copy constructor.
092       *
093       * @param copyFrom The bean to copy from.
094       */
095      protected Builder(HtmlSchemaSerializer copyFrom) {
096         super(copyFrom);
097         generatorBuilder = copyFrom.generator.copy().marshallingContext(marshallingContext());
098      }
099
100      /**
101       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Add descriptions.
102       *
103       * <p>
104       * Identifies which categories of types that descriptions should be automatically added to generated schemas.
105       * <p>
106       * The description is the result of calling {@link ClassMeta#getName()}.
107       *
108       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
109       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#addDescriptionsTo(TypeCategory...)}
110       * </ul>
111       *
112       * @param values
113       *    The values to add to this setting.
114       *    <br>The default is an empty string.
115       * @return This object.
116       */
117      public Builder addDescriptionsTo(TypeCategory...values) {
118         generatorBuilder.addDescriptionsTo(values);
119         return this;
120      }
121
122      /**
123       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Add examples.
124       *
125       * <p>
126       * Identifies which categories of types that examples should be automatically added to generated schemas.
127       * <p>
128       * The examples come from calling {@link ClassMeta#getExample(MarshallingSession,JsonParserSession)} which in turn gets examples
129       * from the following:
130       * <ul class='javatree'>
131       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Example}
132       *    <li class='ja'>{@link Marshalled#example() Marshalled(example)}
133       * </ul>
134       *
135       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
136       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#addExamplesTo(TypeCategory...)}
137       * </ul>
138       *
139       * @param values
140       *    The values to add to this setting.
141       *    <br>The default is an empty string.
142       * @return This object.
143       */
144      public Builder addExamplesTo(TypeCategory...values) {
145         generatorBuilder.addExamplesTo(values);
146         return this;
147      }
148
149      /**
150       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Allow nested descriptions.
151       *
152       * <p>
153       * Identifies whether nested descriptions are allowed in schema definitions.
154       *
155       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
156       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#allowNestedDescriptions()}
157       * </ul>
158       *
159       * @return This object.
160       */
161      public Builder allowNestedDescriptions() {
162         generatorBuilder.allowNestedDescriptions();
163         return this;
164      }
165
166      /**
167       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Allow nested examples.
168       *
169       * <p>
170       * Identifies whether nested examples are allowed in schema definitions.
171       *
172       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
173       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#allowNestedExamples()}
174       * </ul>
175       *
176       * @return This object.
177       */
178      public Builder allowNestedExamples() {
179         generatorBuilder.allowNestedExamples();
180         return this;
181      }
182
183      /**
184       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Schema definition mapper.
185       *
186       * <p>
187       * Interface to use for converting Bean classes to definition IDs and URIs.
188       * <p>
189       * Used primarily for defining common definition sections for beans in Swagger JSON.
190       * <p>
191       * This setting is ignored if {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#useBeanDefs()} is not enabled.
192       *
193       * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
194       *    <li class='jm'>{@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.jsonschema.JsonSchemaGenerator.Builder#beanDefMapper(Class)}
195       * </ul>
196       *
197       * @param value
198       *    The new value for this property.
199       *    <br>The default is {@link BasicBeanDefMapper}.
200       * @return This object.
201       */
202      public Builder beanDefMapper(Class<? extends MarshallingDefMapper> value) {
203         generatorBuilder.beanDefMapper(value);
204         return this;
205      }
206
207      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
208      public HtmlSchemaSerializer build() {
209         return cache(CACHE).build(HtmlSchemaSerializer.class);
210      }
211
212      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
213      public Builder copy() {
214         return new Builder(this);
215      }
216
217      @Override /* Overridden from Context.Builder<?> */
218      public HashKey hashKey() {
219         // @formatter:off
220         return HashKey.of(
221            super.hashKey(),
222            generatorBuilder.hashKey()
223         );
224         // @formatter:on
225      }
226
227      /**
228       * <i><l>HtmlSchemaSerializer</l> configuration property:&emsp;</i>  Use bean definitions.
229       *
230       * <p>
231       * When enabled, schemas on beans will be serialized as the following:
232       * <p class='bjson'>
233       *    {
234       *       type: <js>'object'</js>,
235       *       <js>'$ref'</js>: <js>'#/definitions/TypeId'</js>
236       *    }
237       * </p>
238       *
239       * @return This object.
240       */
241      public Builder useBeanDefs() {
242         generatorBuilder.useBeanDefs();
243         return this;
244      }
245
246
247   }
248
249   /** Default serializer, with whitespace. */
250   public static class Readable extends HtmlSchemaSerializer {
251
252      /**
253       * Constructor.
254       *
255       * @param builder The builder for this object.
256       */
257      public Readable(Builder builder) {
258         super(builder.useWhitespace());
259      }
260   }
261
262   /** Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode. */
263   public static class Simple extends HtmlSchemaSerializer {
264
265      /**
266       * Constructor.
267       *
268       * @param builder The builder for this object.
269       */
270      public Simple(Builder builder) {
271         super(builder.quoteChar('\''));
272      }
273   }
274
275   /** Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode, with whitespace. */
276   public static class SimpleReadable extends HtmlSchemaSerializer {
277
278      /**
279       * Constructor.
280       *
281       * @param builder The builder for this object.
282       */
283      public SimpleReadable(Builder builder) {
284         super(builder.quoteChar('\'').useWhitespace());
285      }
286   }
287
288   /** Default serializer, all default settings.*/
289   public static final HtmlSchemaSerializer DEFAULT = new HtmlSchemaSerializer(create());
290   /** Default serializer, all default settings.*/
291   public static final HtmlSchemaSerializer DEFAULT_READABLE = new Readable(create());
292
293   /** Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode. */
294   public static final HtmlSchemaSerializer DEFAULT_SIMPLE = new Simple(create());
295
296   /** Default serializer, single quotes, simple mode, with whitespace. */
297   public static final HtmlSchemaSerializer DEFAULT_SIMPLE_READABLE = new SimpleReadable(create());
298
299   /**
300    * Creates a new builder for this object.
301    *
302    * @return A new builder.
303    */
304   public static Builder create() {
305      return new Builder();
306   }
307
308   final JsonSchemaGenerator generator;
309
310   /**
311    * Constructor.
312    *
313    * @param builder The builder for this serializer.
314    */
315   public HtmlSchemaSerializer(Builder builder) {
316      super(builder.detectRecursions().ignoreRecursions());
317
318      generator = builder.generatorBuilder.build();
319   }
320
321   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
322   public Builder copy() {
323      return new Builder(this);
324   }
325
326   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
327   public HtmlSchemaSerializerSession.Builder createSession() {
328      return HtmlSchemaSerializerSession.create(this);
329   }
330
331   @Override /* Overridden from Context */
332   public HtmlSchemaSerializerSession getSession() { return createSession().build(); }
333
334   @Override /* Overridden from HtmlSerializer */
335   protected FluentMap<String,Object> properties() {
336      return super.properties()
337         .a(PROP_generator, generator);
338   }
339
340   JsonSchemaGenerator getGenerator() { return generator; }
341}
